title: Lastrea petrophila
taxonomy: Lastrea petrophila is classified under the family Athyriaceae, commonly known as the lady and caddis flies fern family. This classification places it within the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The genus Lastrea is part of a larger group of ferns known as the genus Lastrea, which is a synonym for the genus Athyrium in some sources. However, some sources consider Lastrea petrophila to be a synonym for Athyrium petrophilum. Further research may be needed to determine the most up-to-date classification of Lastrea petrophila.

constituents: Lastrea petrophila, also known as petrophilous lady fern, contains a variety of active constituents. These include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. intro: Lastrea petrophila, also known as Rock Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea petrophila include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rock Fern include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
